text: Dissection of the apex is a critical part of the procedure and avoiding positive surgical margins is crucial for the oncological outcome.
A correct knowledge of the individual anatomical variation of prostatic anatomy is helpful to tailor the correct approach.
On the urethra, the circular striated muscle is incised, revealing the underlying longitudinal smooth muscle.
The urethra can then be divided 3-6 mm distal to the prostatourethral junction to reduce the risk of a positive surgical margin.
Passing the scissors behind the posterior wall of the urethra safely separates it from the rectum.
The posterior aspect of the urethra is then dissected avoiding the use of coagulation.
